reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 51 cfs @ Fort Collins , 1 cfs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir Flows have leveled, and the river is running clear throughout the canyon. This week’s snow has been a welcome surprise up high, and water temperatures will remain cold for the next several days. We’re starting to see the early stages of a light terrestrial bite. On warmer afternoons, consider running a dry-dropper rig. Keep patterns on the smaller side size 12–18 ants, beetles, and smaller hoppers have been effective. Larger stonefly and caddis imitations are also producing. With the recent cold and snow, nymphing has been the most consistent approach. Smaller Blue Wings, midges, and stonefly patterns are all getting it done. A larger stonefly (size 8–12) trailed by a smaller size 16–20 Blue Wing, caddis, or midge has been a go to setup in the canyon. The streamer bite is starting to turn on and is worth mixing in, especially on colder days. Spring runoff is just around the corner stop by the shop to stock up on bigger bugs. We’re getting more guide trips out on the Poudre, so give us a call for the latest conditions and updates. Be sure to check out our Poudre Assortment it's a great selection for this time of year. Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year.
